trust.securityDesc: Your documents are stored securely and permanently deleted after 30 days.
trust.sectionTitle: Built on security, accuracy, and accessibility
trust.sectionSubtitle: Every part of WPO is designed for people who can't afford mistakes with their documents.
trust.pillar1Body: Files stored on Cloudflare R2 with server-side encryption and TLS in transit. Permanently deleted after 30 days. We never share your documents.
trust.pillar2Body: Mistral OCR extracts text from scanned documents. Claude Sonnet translates with context awareness. Names, dates, and numbers are preserved exactly as written.
trust.pillar3Body: Payments are processed securely online. No hidden fees.
trust.paymentDesc: Pay via available payment methods. Fast, secure, no hidden fees.

faq.label: FAQ

faq.q1: Is this translation accepted by consulates or universities?
faq.a1: Our translations are for informational use only. They are not certified or notarized. Always check with your institution whether an unofficial translation is sufficient.
faq.a2: Most documents are translated in 2–5 minutes. Complex or long documents may take up to 10 minutes.
faq.a3: We support Russian, English, Chinese, Korean, Japanese, German, French, Spanish, and Arabic as target languages.
faq.a4: We accept payment via available online payment methods. Check the platform for current options.
faq.a5: Your files are encrypted in transit and at rest. They are permanently deleted after 30 days.

disclaimer: UNOFFICIAL TRANSLATION — FOR INFORMATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY

documentsHub.heroSubheadline: Upload a scanned or digital PDF and receive a clean translated version. Every major official document type supported — passports, bank statements, diplomas, certificates, and more.
documentsHub.finalCtaSub: Upload any official PDF and receive a clean translation in 2–5 minutes.

faq.q: Which document types do you support?
faq.a: We support all major official document types: passports, ID cards, bank statements, diplomas, transcripts, birth and marriage certificates, employment contracts, medical records, police clearance certificates, driver's licenses, and generic official documents.

faq.q: What languages are supported?
faq.a: We support 10+ languages including English, Russian, Chinese, Korean, Japanese, German, French, Spanish, and Arabic. Source language can be auto-detected.

faq.q: Are your translations certified or notarized?
faq.a: No. All translations are unofficial and for informational use only. A visible disclaimer appears on every translated page. Certified or notarized translations must be obtained from a licensed translator or notary.

kazakhstan.heroSubheadline: Translate diplomas, birth certificates, contracts, bank statements, and official documents online. Used by students, migration consultants, notaries, and visa agents for international workflows.
kazakhstan.docsSubheadline: All major document types used in study, migration, and official workflows
kazakhstan.painHeadline: Document translation in Kazakhstan — common bottlenecks
kazakhstan.pain1Title: Translation bureaus charge per page and require physical presence
kazakhstan.pain1Desc: Most certified translation offices in Kazakhstan require you to come in person with originals, wait several days, and pay significant per-page fees for what can be a 2-page diploma.
kazakhstan.pain2Title: Notary appointments need a complete translation draft in advance
kazakhstan.pain2Desc: Many notaries and certified translators in Kazakhstan work faster and more accurately when they have a clean first-pass translation to review, rather than starting from scratch.
kazakhstan.pain3Title: International university applications require precise English formatting
kazakhstan.pain3Desc: Foreign universities evaluating Kazakhstani diplomas want clean, readable English transcriptions — not literal word-for-word renderings that lose structural context.
kazakhstan.pain4Title: Migration and apostille workflows involve many documents at once
kazakhstan.pain4Desc: Preparing for relocation, work abroad, or foreign university admission often means translating 5–10 documents simultaneously. The timeline becomes the main constraint.
kazakhstan.finalCtaHeadline: Translate your documents online — no bureau visit required
kazakhstan.finalCtaSub: Upload your PDF and receive a clean translated version in 2–5 minutes.

faq.q: Can I use this translation before visiting a notary?
faq.a: Yes. Many notaries and certified translators review an informal translation draft before completing the certified version. A clean, structured WPO translation can speed up that conversation and reduce errors in the final certified document.

faq.q: Which languages do you support for Kazakhstan documents?
faq.a: We support Russian ↔ English and English → Russian as the primary pairs. Kazakh ↔ Russian and Kazakh → English are also supported. These cover the main workflows for Kazakhstani documents used internationally.

faq.q: Is this translation accepted by foreign universities?
faq.a: Foreign universities typically require certified or notarized translations. WPO provides unofficial translations that can be used during the application preparation stage, for review by an education consultant, or as a reference draft for a certified translator. Always confirm official requirements with the target university.

faq.q: Can notaries and visa agents use this service on behalf of clients?
faq.a: Yes. Any user can upload a client's document, process it, and receive the translated PDF. A partner dashboard for agencies is planned for a future release.

faq.q: What is an apostille and does translation affect it?
faq.a: An apostille is an official authentication stamp applied to a document by a government authority. The apostille applies to the original document. The translation is a separate step, often required after the apostille has been obtained. WPO provides the translation step; the apostille must be obtained from the relevant government authority.

kazakhstan.pricingSubheadline: No subscription. Pay only for the translation you choose.
kazakhstan.seoHeadline: Document Translation for Kazakhstan — When and How to Use
kazakhstan.seoParagraph1: In Kazakhstan, document translations are often needed for enrolling in foreign universities, work visas, relocation, immigration procedures, opening bank accounts abroad, and apostille. The traditional route through a translation bureau can require a personal visit, waiting time, and manual document handoff.
kazakhstan.seoParagraph2: WorldPrimeOnline helps prepare translations online: upload a document, choose a language pair and service level, and receive the result in electronic format. Electronic translation is suitable for preparation and reference. Official translation with translator review, translator signature, and Provider stamp is available as a separate service level. Notarial certification is arranged separately through partners where available.
kazakhstan.seoParagraph3: Acceptance of translations by third parties is not guaranteed. Before submitting a document to a bank, university, consulate, immigration authority, or notary, verify the requirements of the receiving party.

kazakhstanNotarized.heroSubheadline: Upload your document and receive a clean, structured Russian or English translation ready to present to a notary or certified translator. Reduce review time, reduce errors, lower overall cost.
kazakhstanNotarized.docsHeadline: Document types commonly prepared for notary review
kazakhstanNotarized.painHeadline: Notary translation appointments take longer than necessary
kazakhstanNotarized.pain1Title: Notaries start from scratch on every document
kazakhstanNotarized.pain1Desc: Without a pre-prepared draft, a notary or certified translator reads the original, dictates, and constructs the translation during the appointment — a slow, billable process.
kazakhstanNotarized.pain2Title: Errors in the first draft cost time and money to correct
kazakhstanNotarized.pain2Desc: Name transliterations, date formatting, and institution names are common error points. A pre-processed draft identifies these before the certified version is produced.
kazakhstanNotarized.pain3Title: Multiple documents multiply the wait time
kazakhstanNotarized.pain3Desc: University or migration packages often involve 5–10 documents. Preparing drafts for all of them before one notary session significantly reduces the overall timeline.
kazakhstanNotarized.pain4Title: Clients arrive unprepared, extending appointment duration
kazakhstanNotarized.pain4Desc: For notaries and visa agents, clients who arrive with a clear draft are faster to process. The translated draft also helps clients understand what they are signing.
kazakhstanNotarized.finalCtaHeadline: Prepare your translation before the notary appointment
kazakhstanNotarized.finalCtaSub: Upload your document and receive a structured translation in 2–5 minutes. From 2 290 ₸.

faq.q: Is this a certified or notarized translation?
faq.a: No. WPO provides unofficial translation drafts for informational and preparation purposes. A certified or notarized translation must be produced by a licensed translator or notary. WPO drafts can be used as input to that process, not as a replacement.

faq.q: Will my notary accept a WPO translation draft as a starting point?
faq.a: Most notaries and certified translators are willing to work from a pre-prepared draft. It speeds up their work and reduces the likelihood of errors. However, not all notaries accept external drafts — confirm with your specific notary before using this workflow.

faq.q: What languages are supported?
faq.a: Russian ↔ English, Kazakh → English, and Kazakh → Russian. These cover the main language pairs for documents prepared in Kazakhstan for international use.

faq.q: Can I use this service as a notary or migration consultant?
faq.a: Yes. You can upload client documents and receive translated drafts on their behalf. A dedicated partner workflow is in development.

kazakhstanUniversity.heroSubheadline: Get clean English translations of diplomas, transcripts, school certificates, and academic records for university applications in the UK, US, Europe, Canada, and beyond.
kazakhstanUniversity.heroTrustLine: From 2 590 ₸ · Diploma & Transcript formats · RU / EN
kazakhstanUniversity.painHeadline: University application translation — where it gets complicated
kazakhstanUniversity.pain1Title: International universities require precise academic terminology
kazakhstanUniversity.pain1Desc: A diploma from a Kazakhstani university contains specific field-of-study names, department names, and qualification titles that must be translated using internationally recognised terminology.
kazakhstanUniversity.pain2Title: Transcripts have structured tables — poorly formatted output gets rejected
kazakhstanUniversity.pain2Desc: Academic transcripts contain subject names, credit hours, and grades in tabular format. Generic translation tools convert them to unstructured text. WPO preserves the structure.
kazakhstanUniversity.pain3Title: Tight admission deadlines leave no time for agency delays
kazakhstanUniversity.pain3Desc: University application windows are narrow. Getting a translation in 2–5 minutes versus waiting days from a bureau can determine whether you submit on time.
kazakhstanUniversity.pain4Title: Education consultants need fast drafts for multiple clients at once
kazakhstanUniversity.pain4Desc: Agencies handling multiple university applications simultaneously need a scalable, fast translation tool — not a per-client bureau negotiation.
kazakhstanUniversity.finalCtaSub: Upload your diploma, transcript, or academic certificate and get a clean English translation in minutes.

faq.q: Will foreign universities accept a WPO translation?
faq.a: Requirements vary by university and country. Many universities accept unofficial translations for initial application review, with certified translations required only after acceptance or at enrollment. Always check the specific requirements for your target university.

faq.q: What is a "school leaving certificate" (Attestat)?
faq.a: The Attestat is the Kazakhstani secondary school completion certificate. It lists final grades in all subjects. We translate the complete document including subject names, grades, and qualification details into clean English.

faq.q: Do you preserve the grade scale and grading system?
faq.a: Yes. We preserve the original grades as shown (e.g., 5-point or 100-point scale) and note the grading system in the translated document. We do not convert grades or apply any interpretation.

faq.q: Can you translate documents for IELTS, TOEFL, or credential evaluation purposes?
faq.a: WPO translations are unofficial. They are not accepted by IELTS/TOEFL administrative bodies for formal score reporting. However, they can be used for your own preparation, for informal application review, or as input to a certified translation workflow.

passportTranslation.heroSubheadline: Upload a passport or ID card scan and receive a clean, structured translation. All biographic data, document numbers, dates, and personal details preserved exactly as shown.
passportTranslation.finalCtaSub: Upload your passport scan and receive a clean, structured English translation in minutes.

faq.q: What passport data is included in the translation?
faq.a: We translate all visible fields on the passport data page: surname, given name(s), nationality, date of birth, place of birth, gender, date of issue, date of expiry, passport number, issuing authority, and MRZ zone contents. Personal names are transliterated into the target script.

faq.q: What languages can a passport be translated into?
faq.a: We support 10+ target languages including English, Russian, Chinese, Korean, Japanese, German, French, Spanish, and Arabic. Source language is auto-detected or can be manually specified.

faq.q: Is the translation accepted for visa applications?
faq.a: Our translations are unofficial and for informational use. Some consulates accept informal translated copies alongside the original passport. Others require certified translations. Always confirm with the specific embassy or consulate what level of translation they require.

faq.q: How are names handled in the translation?
faq.a: Personal names are transliterated following ICAO 9303 standards for passport-style documents where applicable, and standard transliteration conventions for other scripts. Names are never translated semantically.

faq.q: Is my passport data safe?
faq.a: Your document is processed and stored on encrypted Cloudflare R2 storage. It is automatically deleted after 30 days. We do not store, share, or use the personal data in your passport for any purpose other than generating the translation.

diplomaTranslation.heroSubheadline: Translate diplomas, degree certificates, and academic transcripts for university applications, professional licensing, credential evaluation, and international employment.
diplomaTranslation.heroTrustLine: 2 590 ₸ per document · Academic terminology preserved · 10+ languages
diplomaTranslation.painHeadline: Diploma translation challenges — what goes wrong
diplomaTranslation.pain1Title: Academic institution names must be translated with care
diplomaTranslation.pain1Desc: University and faculty names have official international forms. Incorrectly translating a university name raises immediate flags with reviewers.
diplomaTranslation.pain2Title: Qualification titles are not universal — context matters
diplomaTranslation.pain2Desc: A Russian 'specialist' degree is not simply a bachelor's degree. Credential evaluators expect accurate description of the qualification type, duration, and field.
diplomaTranslation.pain3Desc: Academic transcripts contain structured course listings with credits, grades, and subject codes. Translation tools that flatten tables into prose produce unusable output.
diplomaTranslation.pain4Title: Tight deadlines for credential evaluation services
diplomaTranslation.pain4Desc: WES, ICAS, and other credential evaluation bodies often have document submission deadlines. Having a fast translation tool available means you can submit on time.
diplomaTranslation.finalCtaHeadline: Translate your diploma or academic transcript
diplomaTranslation.finalCtaSub: Upload your academic credential and receive a clean, structured English translation in minutes.

faq.q: Is your translation accepted for WES or other credential evaluations?
faq.a: WES (World Education Services) and most credential evaluation bodies require certified or notarized translations. WPO provides unofficial translations that can be used during preparation, by education consultants, or as input to a certified translation workflow. Verify official requirements before submitting.

faq.q: Do you translate diploma supplements and grade explanations?
faq.a: Yes. Diploma supplements, grade scale explanations, and attached transcripts are translated as part of the document. Subject names are translated using standard academic English terminology.

faq.q: What languages do you support for diploma translation?
faq.a: We support 10+ languages. The most common pairs for diploma translation are Russian → English, Kazakh → English, and Chinese → English. Source language can be auto-detected.

faq.q: How do you handle degree titles and academic grades?
faq.a: Degree titles are translated with explanatory context where needed (e.g., "Specialist degree (5-year integrated program)"). Grades are preserved in the original scale without conversion.

faq.q: Can I use this for a professional licensing application?
faq.a: Many licensing boards require certified or notarized translations of foreign credentials. WPO provides unofficial translations for preparation and reference. Confirm certification requirements with your licensing board.

bankStatementTranslation.heroSubheadline: Translate bank statements from any language into English or other target languages. All balances, transaction dates, account numbers, and currency amounts preserved exactly as shown.
bankStatementTranslation.heroTrustLine: 2 590 ₸ per document · 2–5 minutes · Any bank, any country
bankStatementTranslation.docsHeadline: Bank and financial document types supported
bankStatementTranslation.painHeadline: Why getting your bank statement translated is harder than it should be
bankStatementTranslation.pain1Title: Visa consulates require English bank statements — most banks don't offer them
bankStatementTranslation.pain1Desc: Banks in Russia, Kazakhstan, and many other countries issue statements only in their local language. Embassies and consulates reviewing visa applications typically require English translations.
bankStatementTranslation.pain2Title: Numbers and amounts must be perfectly preserved — no rounding, no conversion
bankStatementTranslation.pain2Desc: Bank statements used for visa or mortgage applications must show exact original amounts in original currency. Any conversion or rounding creates a discrepancy that can cause rejection.
bankStatementTranslation.pain3Title: Transaction-level detail must be readable and structured
bankStatementTranslation.pain3Desc: A bank statement with 50 transactions must remain a table — not a paragraph. Generic translation tools collapse structure into unformatted text.
bankStatementTranslation.pain4Title: Tight visa deadlines make waiting 3–5 days for a bureau unacceptable
bankStatementTranslation.pain4Desc: DTV, Schengen, and other visa applications have narrow windows. Getting your bank statement translated in minutes, not days, can determine whether your application is ready on time.
bankStatementTranslation.finalCtaHeadline: Translate your bank statement for visa or immigration
bankStatementTranslation.finalCtaSub: Upload your bank statement and receive a clean English translation in 2–5 minutes.

faq.q: Which visa types require a translated bank statement?
faq.a: Schengen visas, UK visitor visas, US B1/B2 visas, and most immigration applications require proof of funds from a bank, typically in English. Other documents like income certificates and payslips are also commonly required.

faq.q: Are currencies and amounts preserved without conversion?
faq.a: Yes. All amounts, currencies, and balances are preserved exactly as they appear in the original document. We do not convert currencies, round amounts, or interpret financial data in any way.

faq.q: What if my bank statement is 6 pages long?
faq.a: We support bank statements up to 50 pages. The price per document applies regardless of page count within that limit. All pages are translated as one document.

faq.q: Will the translated statement be accepted by my consulate?
faq.a: Our translations are unofficial. Many consulates accept informal translations accompanying the original statement. Others require a certified translation. Verify the requirements with your specific consulate or visa agent.

faq.q: Can you translate online banking screenshots or PDF exports?
faq.a: Yes. Both scanned paper statements and digital PDF exports from online banking are supported. Scanned documents are processed through OCR before translation.

tos.title: Terms of Service
tos.s1Body: WPO Translations provides informational translations only. Our translations are produced by artificial intelligence and are intended for informational and review purposes. They are not certified, notarized, sworn, or legally attested translations.
tos.s1Body2: We make no representation that our translations will be accepted by any government body, consulate, court, university, bank, or other institution.
tos.s2Body: Nothing on this platform constitutes legal, immigration, financial, or professional advice. If you need a certified or notarized translation for legal, immigration, or official purposes, please engage a licensed human translator or translation agency.
tos.s3Body: Payments are processed via available payment methods. The price per document translation is displayed at checkout and is subject to change without notice.
tos.s3Body2: No refunds are issued once the translation job has been completed and the translated PDF has been delivered. If you experience a technical failure before delivery, please contact us.
tos.s4Body: Uploaded documents and translated PDFs are stored securely for up to 30 days after the translation is completed, after which they are automatically and permanently deleted.
tos.s5Item1: Upload documents you do not have the right to translate
tos.s5Item2: Use our service to produce fraudulent documents
tos.s5Item3: Attempt to circumvent any security or access controls
tos.s5Item4: Use automated tools to abuse the service
tos.s6Body: We provide the service as-is and make no guarantees of uptime, accuracy, or fitness for any particular purpose.
tos.s7Body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, WPO Translations and its operators shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service or reliance on any translation produced by it.
tos.s8Body: We may update these Terms at any time. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new Terms.
tos.s9Body: For questions about these Terms, please contact us through the website.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.s2: 2. How Long We Keep Your Data
privacyPage.s1Intro: When you use WPO Translations, we collect:
privacyPage.s1Item1: Account information: email address when you sign up
privacyPage.s1Item2: Uploaded documents: PDF files you upload for translation
privacyPage.s1Item3: Translation output: the translated PDFs we produce
privacyPage.s1Item4: Payment information: transaction identifiers and amounts. We do not store payment credentials or private keys.
privacyPage.s1Item5: Usage data: basic logs (timestamps, document types, language pairs) to operate and improve the service
privacyPage.s2Item1: Uploaded and translated files: automatically and permanently deleted 30 days after the translation is completed
privacyPage.s2Item2: Account information: retained until you delete your account
privacyPage.s2Item3: Payment records: retained as required by applicable tax and financial regulations
privacyPage.s3Intro: We use your data exclusively to:
privacyPage.s3Item1: Provide the translation service you requested
privacyPage.s3Item3: Send you the translated document and service notifications
privacyPage.s3Item4: Improve the accuracy and reliability of the service
privacyPage.s4Body: We never sell, rent, or share your documents or personal data with third parties for marketing or advertising purposes.
privacyPage.s4Body2: We use the following sub-processors to operate the service:
privacyPage.s4Item3: Mistral AI — OCR (text extraction from PDFs)
privacyPage.s5Body: All files are stored with encryption at rest. Data in transit is protected by TLS. Access to your documents is restricted to your authenticated account only.
privacyPage.s6Item1: Access the personal data we hold about you
privacyPage.s6Item2: Delete your account and all associated data
privacyPage.s6Item3: Request early deletion of your uploaded documents before the 30-day period
privacyPage.s6Body2: To exercise these rights, contact us through the website. We will respond within 30 days.
privacyPage.s7Body: We use only essential cookies required for authentication (managed by Supabase). We do not use tracking, analytics, or advertising cookies.
privacyPage.s8Body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of significant changes by email or by a notice on the service.
privacyPage.s9Body: For privacy-related questions or data requests, please contact us through the website.

footer.tagline: Professional document translation powered by AI

paymentCompliance.title: Payments
paymentCompliance.processedBy: Card payments are processed through Halyk Bank JSC internet acquiring service — Halyk ePay.
paymentCompliance.processedByPending: Card payments will be processed through Halyk Bank JSC internet acquiring service — Halyk ePay (pending gateway activation).
paymentCompliance.threeDSecure: Card transactions may be authenticated with 3D Secure technology where required by the issuing bank or payment system.
paymentCompliance.deliveryCost: Electronic delivery cost: 0 ₸ (included in document price).

serviceTerms.deliveryBody: Translated documents are delivered electronically as PDF or DOCX files. Delivery time: from 2–5 minutes to 2 business days, depending on document type, volume, service level, and quality of the uploaded file.
serviceTerms.officialNote: Official translation with human translator review may take up to 5 business days.
serviceTerms.notarizationNote: Notarization through certified partners, where available, is a separate service and requires additional time.
serviceTerms.cancellationBody: You may cancel an order before processing starts and receive a full refund. Once OCR or translation has started, the Refund Policy applies.
serviceTerms.technicalFaultBody: If the service cannot be delivered due to a technical fault on the Provider's side, a full refund or free reprocessing will be offered.
serviceTerms.poorQualityBody: Poor scan quality, incorrect language selection, or unreadable files may limit translation quality or require reprocessing, which may affect delivery time.
serviceTerms.reprocessingBody: If the translation result contains a technical error attributable to the service, you may request free correction or reprocessing.
serviceTerms.reprocessingQualityLimit: Translation quality depends on source document quality. The Provider is not liable for inaccuracies caused by OCR errors on low-quality source files.

pain.label: THE PROBLEM
pain.title: Translation bureaus weren't designed for your deadline
pain.item1Desc: Traditional bureaus work during office hours. Your deadline does not.
pain.item2Desc: Files sent over WhatsApp and email. No tracking. No transparency.
pain.item3Desc: Per-page, per-language, per-urgency. Final price surprises you at the end.
pain.item4Desc: The bureau is in another city or country. You need the document today.

security.label: PRIVACY AND SECURITY
security.title: Your documents stay private
security.subtitle: We handle passports, bank statements, and medical records. Security is not optional.
security.item1Desc: Cloudflare R2 with server-side encryption and TLS in transit.
security.item2Desc: Original and translated files are permanently deleted after 30 days. No archives.
security.item3Desc: Translation is performed by AI only. No staff reads your documents.
security.item4Desc: You can request immediate deletion from your dashboard at any time.
security.disclaimer: UNOFFICIAL TRANSLATION — FOR INFORMATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY
security.disclaimerSub: Not certified or notarized. Always verify with the receiving institution.

useCases.bankingDesc: Bank statements, employment contracts, and proof of income documents.
useCases.relocationDesc: Any official document you need to translate to live, work, or register abroad.

kazakhstanCertified.heroSubheadline: Order an official translation with translator review, translator signature, and Provider stamp. For banks, universities, migration, visa, and official purposes.
kazakhstanCertified.heroTrustLine: Acceptance of the translation by third parties is not guaranteed and depends on the requirements of the receiving organization.
kazakhstanCertified.painHeadline: Official Translation — a Separate Service Level
kazakhstanCertified.pain1Desc: The translation is reviewed by a human translator. OCR errors, terminology, and formatting are corrected as needed.
kazakhstanCertified.pain2Desc: The final document may include a translator signature and translator block.
kazakhstanCertified.pain3Desc: For the official service level, a Provider stamp may be added to the document.

kazakhstanCertified.finalCtaSub: Upload a document, choose the official service level, and review the final price before payment.

faq.q: What does certified translation mean at WPO?
faq.a: At WPO, certified translation means the official service level: the translation is reviewed by a human translator and may include a translator signature, Provider stamp, date, and translator block. It does not automatically mean notarial certification or guaranteed acceptance by third parties.

faq.q: How does certified translation differ from electronic translation?
faq.a: Electronic translation is performed automatically using OCR and AI, without a translator signature, Provider stamp, or notarial certification. Certified / official translation undergoes translator review and may include a translator signature and Provider stamp.

faq.q: Is this a notarized translation?
faq.a: No. WPO certified / official translation is not notarial certification by itself. Notarization is arranged separately through a partner process, if this service is available for the selected document, language pair, and jurisdiction.

faq.q: Do you guarantee that the translation will be accepted by a bank, university, or consulate?
faq.a: No. Acceptance of a translation depends on the requirements of the specific bank, university, consulate, immigration authority, notary, or other organization. Before using the translation for official purposes, the customer must independently verify the requirements of the receiving party.

faq.q: When is an official translation needed?
faq.a: An official translation is usually needed when the receiving party requires a translation with translator review, translator signature, Provider stamp, or additional confirmation. If a standard electronic translation is insufficient, choose the official service level.

faq.q: Can notarial certification be added later?
faq.a: In some cases yes, if a partner process is available for the selected document, language, and jurisdiction. Availability, timeline, and cost of notarization are confirmed separately.
